behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at the embedding-model output boundary: a real model/version swap can change vector length and break indexing even if local wiring is correct. A mock embedding client can hide this by always returning expected length (MOCK-HIDE). Real external call is involved, but correctness here depends on actual AI/ML output shape, so Agentic is the minimum. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior text is single-step ('embedding vector produced ... dimensionality'), not a multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run against the real embedding model endpoint configured for production-like use; issue a real query string, assert returned vector length equals the live index dimension from the vector DB schema/config, and fail on mismatch.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is real vector-store/query behavior (index metric config, DB-side sorting/ANN return ordering), which often appears only with the actual DB process/API. In-process mocks can always return sorted fixtures and miss real ordering defects (MOCK-HIDE, REAL-INFRA). Rejected higher level (Agentic): behavior text is about cosine-score ordering, not AI judgment quality; it can be validated with fixed vectors and known scores.
behaviors.test_description: Start a real vector DB instance, load deterministic vectors, execute the actual similarity-search path, and assert returned items are monotonically non-increasing by cosine similarity score.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly tests model decision quality/behavior under nuanced intent; stubs make it trivially pass or fail and cannot validate true reranking judgment (LLM-DEP, deterministic-assertion trap). Rejected higher level (Workflow): the behavior targets one model decision point ('LLM re-ranking step changes the order'), not multiple agentic stages requiring sequential validation.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a fixed candidate list and a nuanced query, call the real LLM reranker, and assert output order differs from raw similarity order for at least one position across repeated runs with acceptance criteria tolerant to some variance.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Whether a natural-language query has 'no close matches' depends on real embedding semantics and retrieval relevance, so a fake embedding can hide production noise behavior (LLM/ML dependence). This is not just local threshold code unless behavior were explicitly narrowed to deterministic score filtering. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior text is single-outcome retrieval emptiness, not a required chain of multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use a corpus intentionally unrelated to the test query, run real query embedding + retrieval path, and assert final returned set is empty (or below strict relevance threshold) instead of returning unrelated items.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: This is full-pipeline SLO behavior across sequential steps (embedding model call -> vector search -> LLM rerank). Median latency only emerges with real orchestration and multiple agentic/external components together; lower-level tests miss interaction and queuing effects (MULTI-STEP + REAL-INFRA + LLM-DEP).
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like end-to-end benchmark over representative queries through the live API path (real embedding service, real vector DB, real LLM reranker), collect latency distribution, and assert p50 < 2.0s.
